description: Three-tier RBAC system where permissions are granted through roles assigned at system, workspace, and channel scopes. Roles are additive and hierarchical.

actors.name: System Administrator
actors.description: Holds unrestricted access; manages all roles and permissions globally

actors.name: Team Administrator
actors.description: Manages roles and members within a specific team

actors.name: Channel Administrator
actors.description: Manages roles within a specific channel

actors.name: Regular Member
actors.description: Standard user with team and channel-level permissions

actors.name: Guest User
actors.description: Restricted participant with limited cross-channel visibility

fields.name: permissions
fields.label: Array of permission IDs granted by this role

fields.name: scheme_managed
fields.label: True if the role is created and managed by a permission scheme

fields.name: built_in
fields.label: True for platform-defined roles that cannot be deleted

values.name: active
values.description: Role is available for assignment; delete_at is zero

values.name: deleted
values.description: Custom role soft-deleted; assignments preserved but ignored

transitions.description: System admin deletes a custom (non-built-in) role

rules.rule_01: Three permission scopes exist: system (platform-wide), team (per workspace), and channel (per channel). Permissions granted at higher scopes apply to lower scopes.
rules.rule_02: Built-in roles (system_admin, system_user, team_admin, team_user, channel_admin, channel_user, guest variants) cannot be deleted.
rules.rule_03: Scheme-managed roles are controlled by permission schemes and cannot be directly reassigned via explicit role assignment.
rules.rule_04: A user holds a system role (from their user record), zero or more team roles (from each team membership), and zero or more channel roles (from each channel membership).
rules.rule_05: Permission resolution is additive: if any role at any scope grants a permission, access is allowed.
rules.rule_06: A system administrator bypasses all permission checks unless the RestrictSystemAdmin setting is enabled.
rules.rule_07: A member cannot simultaneously hold both the user and guest roles within the same team or channel.
rules.rule_08: Explicit roles (non-scheme roles) can be assigned in addition to scheme-assigned defaults.
rules.rule_09: Role permission updates take effect immediately for all users currently holding that role.

given: actor requests access to a resource or action
actor holds at least one role that includes the required permission at the relevant scope
actor requests access to a resource or action
no role held by the actor at any scope grants the required permission
permission_denied.result: Access denied; action blocked with permission error

given: permissions list contains only valid permission IDs

then.description: Custom role persisted with given permissions

given: role exists and is not a scheme-managed role being modified outside a scheme
role_updated.result: Permission change takes effect immediately for all role holders

given: actor attempts to delete a role with built_in or scheme_managed flag set
actor has permission to manage team roles
target user is an active member of the team
new roles are valid and compatible (not both user and guest)

then.description: Explicit roles updated for team membership record

team_member_role_assigned.result: Role change reflected immediately in permission checks for that team

given: actor attempts to assign both guest and user roles to the same member
guest_user_role_conflict.result: Assignment rejected; a member may only be guest or user, not both

errors.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.
The specified role does not exist.
Built-in and scheme-managed roles cannot be deleted.
A role with that name already exists.
One or more permission IDs are not valid for this scope.
A member cannot simultaneously hold both guest and user roles.

events.name: rbac.role_created
events.description: A new custom role was created

events.name: rbac.role_updated
events.description: A role's permission set was modified

events.name: rbac.permission_checked
events.description: An authorization check was performed (for audit/observability)

events.name: rbac.team_member_role_changed
events.description: A team member's roles were updated

events.name: rbac.channel_member_role_changed
events.description: A channel member's roles were updated

related.reason: Teams are the primary boundary where team-scoped roles are applied
Schemes define default role assignments for teams and channels
Guest role is a special system-level role with restricted defaults
Channel moderation patches role permissions at the channel scope

goals.description: Three-tier RBAC system where permissions are granted through roles assigned at system, workspace, and channel scopes. Roles are additive and hierarchical.

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: access control must enforce least-privilege principle

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
state transitions follow the defined state machine — no illegal transitions
